title: Infernal Warden (Game)
summary: We had a small Game Jam at the course just over Easter
details: Just around Easter, we got a challenge to do a small Game Jam, giving us about a week to create a game
I chose to go with Phaser engine to make it as simple as possible.
I quickly settled on a theme and genre, and went to work fleshing out the logic and gameflow with the help of LLMs.
I did spend the majority of the time writing all the logic and functionality, leaving me with a small amount of time to do some basic art.
Unfortunately, it also meant I didn't have time to do any sounds or music.
But the Game Jam ultimately left me inspired to build upon it, as I plan to make it into a proper 3D game later

title: GO JSON Server
summary: Wanting to expand my knowledge, I ended up studying GO, and making a 'fake' JSON Server with it.
details: Using Typicode's 'Fake JSON' Server as a starting point, I wanted to recreate it in GO to get some experience in GO.
I also wanted it to be dependency free, only utilizing the standard GO library.

title: Service to Service API
summary: A small S2S API for entertainment and media backlogging
details: At the end of December we got personalized projects to do.
As I expressed an interest in backend and logic, I got the task to make a service to service API.
I chose to go with an entertainment and media backlog service, letting the user of the API create personalized lists of media and entertainment they have on their 'backlog'
For more of a challenge, the course supervisor also told me to utilize TypeScript and do it in more of a test driven development fashion.

title: Hunt the wumpus group project
summary: Replicating and reimagining an old game called 'Hunt The Wumpus'
details: The first proper group project we had, where we had dedicated roles.
I ended up doing some logic and some project management, doing A-Star Path finding for generating the maps.
as well as retrofitting the new A-Star path finding algorithm to work with the backend another guy had created.
The project ended up being more of a learning experience for me I suppose, and the project never really saw full completion unfortunately.

title: This Portfolio
summary: An ongoing project for several months using React & Tailwind
details: I decided to go with React + vite (JS) with Tailwindcss for my Portfolio as we had just been learning React when we were gonna start building our Portfolios.
The design part of the portfolio started in August of 2025, using Figma to set up Wireframes and over the course of the month experimenting and building out our designs.
Portfolios are ever evolving, so even though I've made it during my stay at Kodehode, I'll still continue to update and tweak it going forward.

title: Gutendex React App
summary: As the final React assingment we were tasked to make a book finder app using the Gutendex API

title: Match 2 React App
summary: As a project spanning a couple of days, we were told to make a match 2 memory game using React

summary: I decided to go with a recipe archive when we got an assignment to utilize LocalStorage

title: To-do list
summary: Extra Assingment to make a todo-list using an API
details: We had the option to take some extra assignments during the course, and this is one of them.
We were given a couple of JavaScript files to utilize to manipulate the DOM to dynamically populate a pre-set to-do list based on some fetched data.

title: Craftsvilla
summary: Progress after about one month into the course
details: This was an assignment after about a month into Kodehode's curriculum, having being taught HTML and basic CSS at this point.
We were asked to design a multi-page website for a fictional company called Craftsvilla, which was all about creativity and arts

title: First Assignment
summary: My first assigment done at Kodehode - Where my journey began.
details: Where my journey at Kodehode began at the end of May 2025.
Coming into the web developer course, I had some basic knowledge of HTML, CSS & JS after having gone through freecodecamp's curriculum a bit a year prior.
I started focusing on Python once I got to that part of their curriculum, so most of this project were done based on googling and the small amount of lectures we had during the first week.
The difference between my first week and now is quite stark - I've learned a lot during my time at Kodehode!
